text:
Elon Musk’s acquisition of Mesh puts the entire optical-interconnect sector on the strategic map.

Mesh builds the optical systems.

Sivers $SIVE develops the high-performance lasers and photonics technology that can power them. https://t.co/n7EwBJh4WN

Here is what Putin spoke:
"I am pleased to welcome the participants and organizers of the 13th Forum of Regions of Russia and Belarus, which traditionally takes place on the eve of Independence Day of the Republic of Belarus, celebrated on July 3 and marking the anniversary of Minsk's liberation from the Nazi invaders.

I sincerely congratulate all Belarusian friends on this national holiday. Russia will always remember that during the Great Patriotic War, our peoples, shoulder to shoulder and at enormous sacrifice, defended freedom and independence, bringing peace to Europe and all of humanity.

It is important that Russia and Belarus carefully preserve and enhance their fraternal traditions of friendship and mutual assistance, strengthening their historically established cultural and spiritual ties. Russian-Belarusian relations are truly strategic, and our Union State serves as a model of mutually beneficial and equal integration, effectively facilitating the deepening of cooperation within such associations as the Eurasian Economic Union, the Commonwealth of Independent States, and the Collective Security Treaty Organization.

One of the main driving forces behind the partnership between Russia and Belarus, as well as the construction of the Union State, are ties between regions. This is the solid foundation upon which cooperation between the two countries is steadily developing across a wide range of areas—political and security, social and humanitarian, and, of course, economic.

Forums of Regions regularly bring together representatives of federal and local authorities, legislators and businesspeople, experts, and public figures—in short, all those who help establish and develop direct contacts between Russian and Belarusian regions.

This year, more than 600 delegates from 73 constituent entities of the Russian Federation arrived in the capital of Belarus. Among them are governors, heads of regional legislative assemblies, deputies, ministers, and company executives. The Belarusian delegation is no less prestigious.

The substantial package of agreements and commercial contracts signed during the forum demonstrates our mutual focus on achieving concrete results.

You are discussing a truly relevant topic: "Economic Interaction between Regions – the Foundation for Sustainable Development of Russia and Belarus." I note that, thanks in large part to close interregional economic cooperation, bilateral trade turnover last year broke another record – $52 billion, according to our statistics. This year, growth continues – more than 7 percent in the first quarter. All mutual settlements between our countries are conducted in national currencies.

The harmonization of legislation in trade, investment, and financial regulation is actively underway. Approaches in customs and tax matters are being unified, and a unified quality control system for goods is being implemented. The Union State Committee on Standardization and Quality held its first meeting during the forum.

Russia leads the way in accumulated investment in the Belarusian economy, at approximately $4 billion. Companies with Russian capital operate in all regions of the republic, with approximately 2,500 of them nationwide.

The regions are closely involved in the implementation of 26 joint import substitution projects in high-tech sectors. For example, modern mining equipment is being manufactured with Belarusian partners in the Moscow Region, machine tool components are being produced in the Novosibirsk Region, and equipment from the Minsk Tractor Plant is being assembled in Tatarstan.

Production cooperation has been established between Belarusian civil aviation plants and the interregional cluster of the Irkutsk, Ulyanovsk, Nizhny Novgorod, and Voronezh Regions, which produces the MS-21 and Sukhoi Superjet 100 airliners. Design bureaus in the Sverdlovsk and Brest Regions are working on the Osvey light multirole aircraft project.

Leading Belarusian factories Integral and Planar supply components for electronics manufacturing in Tatarstan and the Nizhny Novgorod and Moscow regions. Companies in Moscow, St. Petersburg, and Minsk have established effective cooperation chains for the production of optical devices and photonics instruments for communications, data transmission, and IT systems."

Regular passenger flights operate from 10 Russian regions to Belarus. Cross-border commuter trains between Smolensk and Vitebsk and between Smolensk and Orsha were launched in April. Service between Velikiye Luki and Vitebsk, Velikiye Luki and Polotsk, and Bryansk and Gomel are next in line. Plans include the construction of a high-speed railway between Moscow and Minsk.

The railway and port infrastructure of Russian regions is being used to transport Belarusian exports. A permit-free transit procedure is being gradually introduced for Russian and Belarusian goods transported to third countries by road.

text:
$SIVE   AI분석

1. $SIVE (Sivers Semiconductors) 핵심 사업과 최근 뉴스
Sivers Semiconductors는 스웨덴 회사로, 크게 두 축을 가지고 있습니다:
•  Photonics 부문: InP 기반 고출력·협대역 CW DFB 레이저와 레이저 어레이. AI 데이터센터 광연결(CPO), FMCW LiDAR에 특화.
•  Wireless 부문: mmWave 빔포머 IC (5G, Satcom, defense).
2026년 3월 발표된 LiDAR 뉴스가 정확히 사용자가 말한 내용입니다:
•  전략적 LiDAR 고객이 자사 기술을 플랫폼 전체에 채택.
•  2026년 Q4부터 생산 ramp 시작 (자동차 + 산업/로보틱스용).
•  공급 품목: CW DFB 레이저 + 광증폭기.
•  누적 수익 잠재력 $53M ~ $138M (고객 제품 수명 주기 기준).
이 고객은 공식 확인은 안 됐지만, 시장에서 Aeva로 강력하게 추정되고 있습니다. 이유:
•  Aeva는 FMCW LiDAR 전문 (협대역·고출력 레이저가 핵심).
•  Aeva + LG Innotek 전략 파트너십 (2025년 7월 발표, LG Innotek이 Aeva 모듈 제조·공급).
•  Aeva는 로보틱스·휴머노이드·산업 자동화도 타겟팅하고 있음 (Boston Dynamics Atlas 같은 고급 비전 시스템에 잘 맞는 기술).
이게 바로 사용자가 말한 “$AEVA와 LG Innotek을 통해 공급망으로 들어온다”는 부분입니다. 2026년 말 생산 확대 + 상당한 수익 잠재력도 정확히 맞아요.
2. $SPCX (SpaceX 프록시) Mesh Optical 인수와의 연결고리
최근 뉴스 (2026년 6월 26일경): Musk/SpaceX 측이 Mesh Optical Technologies 인수에 FTC 승인 받음. Mesh는 전 SpaceX 엔지니어들이 만든 스타트업으로, AI 데이터센터용 1.6Tbps 광 트랜시버를 개발 중입니다. DFB 레이저를 사용하고, flip-chip 패키징 등 고효율 광 인터커넥트 기술.
연결 포인트:
•  Mesh가 필요로 하는 핵심 부품 중 하나가 고성능 DFB 레이저 → Sivers가 정확히 만드는 제품.
•  Sivers는 이미 Ayar Labs, POET Technologies 등 CPO(코패키지드 옵틱스) 플레이어에 공급 중.
•  SpaceX/Starlink 생태계(레이저 기반 위성 간 통신) + xAI/Tesla 데이터센터 수요 증가 → 광·포토닉스 전반 수요 확대.
•  X(트위터)에서도 최근 “Mesh 인수 후 SIVE가 레이저 공급으로 수혜”라는 언급이 나오고 있어요.
결론: LiDAR 뉴스 자체가 Mesh 인수와 “직접” 연결되는 건 아니지만, 전체 포토닉스/AI 인프라/Satcom 테마에서 시너지 효과가 분명히 있습니다. $SIVE가 “포토닉스 + LiDAR + Satcom” 3박자 비대칭 플레이인 건 맞아요.
3. 주가 상황과 투자 포인트
•  52주 범위: 2.85 SEK ~ 110 SEK (고점 6월 2일).
•  현재 (6월 26~27일경): 약 63 SEK 수준 → 고점 대비 약 42~43% 하락.
•  YTD +1,400% 이상, 1년 +1,500% 이상 폭등 후 조정. “2000% 올랐으니 기다리자” 하던 사람들 입장에서는 정확히 조정 구간.
강점 (Bull case):
•  AI 데이터센터 광연결(CPO) + 로보틱스 LiDAR + Satcom 수요가 동시에 터질 수 있는 희귀한 포지션.
•  Opportunity pipeline 이미 $799M (2025년 말 대비 +77%).
•  기술적으로 InP DFB 레이저에서 경쟁력 있음 (특히 narrow linewidth + high power).
리스크 (Bear case, 상당히 큼):
•  극단적 고평가: trailing P/S 60x 이상. 많은 분석가들이 “과대평가”라고 지적.
•  아직 적자 지속 (Q1 2026 revenue SEK 61.9M, 전년比 -22%, 손실 확대). 실행 리스크 높음.
•  고객 집중 리스크, ramp 지연 가능성.
•  과거 희석 이력 있음. 소형주(시총 약 18~19B SEK)라 변동성 극심.
•  경쟁사: Coherent, Lumentum 등 대형 플레이어 존재.
종합 의견
$SIVE에 극도로 bullish할 만한 근거는 충분합니다. 특히:
•  Aeva/LG Innotek 공급망 + 2026년 Q4 ramp
•  Mesh Optical 인수로 인한 포토닉스 테마 강화
•  AI + 로보틱스 + Satcom 3중 수혜 가능성
고점 대비 40% 조정한 지금이 “겁먹고 포기할 타이밍”은 아니에요. 다만 고위험 speculative 종목이라는 점을 명확히 해야 합니다. valuation이 이미 많은 성공을 선반영하고 있어서, ramp가 늦어지거나 기대가 어긋나면 추가 조정도 충분히 나올 수 있어요.
지금 사야 할까?
→ 장기적으로 photonics/AI 인프라를 강하게 믿고, 변동성을 감당할 수 있다면 분할 매수 관점에서 관심 가질 만한 구간입니다.
→ 단기 트레이딩이나 “무조건 오른다” 마인드라면 리스크가 매우 큽니다.

text:
$SIVE is down here not because of anything Sivers did. 

The entire photonics complex is in a drawdown, and that's exactly the kind of dislocation worth leaning into.

What actually happened:
On June 9, @SemiAnalysis_ published an institutional-only note flagging potential delays in CPO deployment, with a yield-based bear argument (their math: 95% per-engine yield compounded across 32 optical engines = ~19% system yield). 

The note hit institutional desks first. The whole optical group sold off together: $AAOI -14%, $COHR -11%, $POET -12%, $LITE -8%, $MRVL -7.6%, and $SIVE -8.7% alongside them.

Here's the tell. Under the exact same negative news, $AAOI fell ~17% intraday while Lumentum, with a highly overlapping technology profile, fell ~8%. Roughly a 2x difference in decline for two names exposed to the identical headline. 

That spread is the signature of a positioning-driven shakeout flushing out the loosest hands (high retail concentration, high margin balances), not a calm fundamental repricing. 

The names that bled most were the ones with the loosest float, not the ones with the worst fundamentals.

This hit a sector that was up triple digits YTD. $AAOI +390%, $MRVL +228%, $LITE +138%, $COHR +107%, $POET +100%. $SOXX dropped while the $VIX barely moved. That combination, sharp single-name drawdowns with no systemic panic, is profit-taking and sector rotation off parabolic runs, not a demand collapse.

And on the other side of the same week: $NVDA's own blog states Spectrum-X Ethernet Photonics is now in full production, with CPO-based next-gen Spectrum-X switching going into scale-out and scale-across deployments on the Vera Rubin platform. 

The demand signal didn't break. A timing-and-magnitude call on the ramp got mistranslated by the time it reached retail into "CPO is cancelled," which it isn't.

A CPO timing delay does not threaten the Sivers thesis the way it threatens a name priced for imminent volume.

Sivers isn't trading on 2026 CPO revenue. The CPO ramp was always a 2027 qualification, 2028 volume story. 

Even @aleabitoreddit, asked directly if the selloff shook it's faith in photonics, said they're more bullish as prices drop.

When a quality supply-chain position gets sold purely because it shares a sector ETF with the names institutions are trimming, that's the dislocation you want. 

Buying the bottleneck while the complex flushes weak hands is a better entry than chasing it green. 

Just size it as the high-beta position it is, because the volatility that created this entry cuts both ways.

If you are still bullish on $SIVE - drop a like!

Not Financial Advice. DYOR.

The entire photonics complex is in a drawdown, and that's exactly the kind of dislocation worth leaning into. What actually happened: On June 9, @SemiAnalysis_ published an institutional-only note flagging potential delays in CPO deployment, with a yield-based bear argument (their math: 95% per-engine yield compounded across 32 optical engines = ~19% system yield). The note hit institutional desks first. The whole optical group sold off together: $AAOI -14%, $COHR -11%, $POET -12%, $LITE -8%, $MRVL -7.6%, and $SIVE -8.7% alongside them. Here's the tell. Under the exact same negative news, $AAOI fell ~17% intraday while Lumentum, with a highly overlapping technology profile, fell ~8%. Roughly a 2x difference in decline for two names exposed to the identical headline. That spread is the signature of a positioning-driven shakeout flushing out the loosest hands (high retail concentration, high margin balances), not a calm fundamental repricing. The names that bled most were the ones with the loosest float, not the ones with the worst fundamentals. This hit a sector that was up triple digits YTD. $AAOI +390%, $MRVL +228%, $LITE +138%, $COHR +107%, $POET +100%. $SOXX dropped while the $VIX barely moved. That combination, sharp single-name drawdowns with no systemic panic, is profit-taking and sector rotation off parabolic runs, not a demand collapse. And on the other side of the same week: $NVDA's own blog states Spectrum-X Ethernet Photonics is now in full production, with CPO-based next-gen Spectrum-X switching going into scale-out and scale-across deployments on the Vera Rubin platform. The demand signal didn't break. A timing-and-magnitude call on the ramp got mistranslated by the time it reached retail into "CPO is cancelled," which it isn't. A CPO timing delay does not threaten the Sivers thesis the way it threatens a name priced for imminent volume. Sivers isn't trading on 2026 CPO revenue. The CPO ramp was always a 2027 qualification, 2028 volume story. Even @aleabitoreddit, asked directly if the selloff shook it's faith in photonics, said they're more bullish as prices drop. When a quality supply-chain position gets sold purely because it shares a sector ETF with the names institutions are trimming, that's the dislocation you want. Buying the bottleneck while the complex flushes weak hands is a better entry than chasing it green. Just size it as the high-beta position it is, because the volatility that created this entry cuts both ways. If you are still bullish on $SIVE - drop a like! Not Financial Advice. text:
The $QQQ as launched in March of 1999.  The ETFs always arrive at exactly the wrong time, enabling the masses to plow into the most bloated, overvalued themes with ease and at little cost.  

How does this look for AI?  You were early in mid 2023 and 2024.  Then in 2025 you were in the middle innings.  And now you are late anon, with many launches per month popping up in AI and related themes.  

Everyone is in, and everyone is an expert. 

Dozens of AI-related ETFs have launched in the past 3 years (roughly mid-2023 to mid-2026), contributing to a total of over 90 AI-themed ETFs now available in the US. 
Key Context on Launches
•  Pre-2023 baseline: A smaller number existed (e.g., broader robotics/AI funds like BOTZ or AIQ from earlier years). The post-ChatGPT AI boom (late 2022 onward) triggered a sharp increase in new, targeted launches. 
•  Notable recent examples:
•  2023: Roundhill Generative AI & Technology ETF (CHAT, launched May 2023) — one of the first pure generative AI-focused funds. 
•  2024–2026: A wave including iShares A.I. Innovation and Tech Active ETF (BAI, Oct 2024), various Global X, Invesco, WisdomTree, First Trust, and niche products (e.g., memory/semiconductor photonics, pure-play AI infrastructure like DRAM in 2026). Multiple launches per month in peak periods, often active or thematic.

text:
앞으로 엔비디아 주가나 반도체 패권을 결정할 진짜 무기는 GPU가 아니라 '빛'일지도 모릅니다.

 최근 엔비디아가 왜 구리선 대신 반도체 칩 안에 '레이저(빛)'를 집어넣는 기술에만 무려 65억 달러(9조 원) 넘는 돈을 쏟아부었을까요?

 반도체 판을 통째로 바꿀 '빛의 속도' 전쟁

지금 반도체 공학자들이 마주한 가장 큰 벽은 의외로 연산 속도가 아닙니다. 

바로 우리가 늘 쓰는 '구리선(전기 신호)'의 물리적 한계예요.

지금까지 칩과 칩은 구리선으로 데이터를 주고받았는데, 

AI 시대가 오면서 데이터가 폭발하니까 구리선이 버티질 못하는 상황이 온 거죠.

전기를 너무 많이, 빠르게 흘리니까 불타오르듯 열이 나고(발열), 전기 신호가 구리선 중심이 아니라 '겉표면'으로만 흐르려고 해서 속도가 뚝 떨어지는 병목 현상이 생깁니다.

쉽게 말해 경부고속도로에 갑자기 수백만 대의 슈퍼카가 몰려와서 도로 전체가 주차장으로 변하고 엔진이 과열되는 상태인 거죠.

"야, 이대로 가다간 데이터센터 전기세 감당도 안 되고 칩이 다 타 죽겠다!" 

그래서 나온 천재적인 치트키가 바로 '실리콘 포토닉스(Silicon Photonics)'입니다.

쉽게 말해, 

우리가 국가 간 인터넷 연결할 때 쓰는 거대한 '광케이블'을 아주 미세하게 축소해서 반도체 칩 안에 통째로 집어넣는 기술이에요. 

전기가 아니라 빛으로 대화하는 칩인 셈이죠.

이게 왜 대박이냐면, 구리선은 선 하나당 신호 하나만 갈 수 있잖아요? 

근데 빛은 '색상(파장)'만 다르면 하나의 통로에서 수십 개의 신호가 섞이지 않고 동시에 달릴 수 있어요.

길을 물리적으로 넓히지 않아도 차선이 무한대로 늘어나는 마법 같은 효과가 생기는 겁니다. 속도가 수십 배 뛰는 건 당연하고요.

게다가 빛은 마찰열(저항)이 아예 없습니다. 

AI 데이터센터에서 가장 골치 아픈 '발열'과 이를 식히기 위한 '냉각 비용'을 최대 30~40%까지 날려버릴 수 있다는 뜻이죠.

실제로 엔비디아가 올해(2026년) 들어서만

 코히어런트, 루멘텀 같은 광학·유리 소재 기업들에 조 단위 투자를 감행한 이유가 여기 있습니다. 

구리선으로는 수백만 대의 GPU를 하나로 묶는 게 불가능하다는 걸 알기 때문이죠.

그럼 이 엄청난 칩은 대체 누가 만들까요?

 결국 파운드리와 패키징 싸움입니다.

TSMC는 전기 칩 위에 광학 칩을 아파트처럼 수직으로 완벽하게 쌓아 올리는 'COUPE'라는 자체 플랫폼을 만들어서 올해 본격적인 양산 카운트다운에 들어갔습니다. 

반도체 제조의 중심축이 미세 공정을 넘어 '광학 패키징'으로 이동하고 있는 국면이에요.

요약하자면, 

HBM 다음 타자로 반도체 시장의 판돈을 싹 쓸어 담을 핵심 키워드는 '실리콘 포토닉스'입니다. 

엔비디아의 독주를 막으려는 빅테크나, 이를 추격하는 파운드리 기업들에게는 절대 놓칠 수 없는 마지막 열쇠인 셈이죠.

앞으로 반도체 뉴스 보실 때 '광학', 'CPO', '실리콘 포토닉스'라는 단어가 보이면 무조건 주목해 보세요.

"Tm90ZVR3ZWV0OjIwNzIxMDQ0MTEyNzE0NDI0MzI=", "media": {"inline_media": []}, "richtext": {"richtext_tags": []}, "text": "앞으로 엔비디아 주가나 반도체 패권을 결정할 진짜 무기는 GPU가 아니라 '빛'일지도 모릅니다. 최근 엔비디아가 왜 구리선 대신 반도체 칩 안에 '레이저(빛)'를 집어넣는 기술에만 무려 65억 달러(9조 원) 넘는 돈을 쏟아부었을까요? 반도체 판을 통째로 바꿀 '빛의 속도' 전쟁 지금 반도체 공학자들이 마주한 가장 큰 벽은 의외로 연산 속도가 아닙니다. 바로 우리가 늘 쓰는 '구리선(전기 신호)'의 물리적 한계예요. 지금까지 칩과 칩은 구리선으로 데이터를 주고받았는데, AI 시대가 오면서 데이터가 폭발하니까 구리선이 버티질 못하는 상황이 온 거죠. 전기를 너무 많이, 빠르게 흘리니까 불타오르듯 열이 나고(발열), 전기 신호가 구리선 중심이 아니라 '겉표면'으로만 흐르려고 해서 속도가 뚝 떨어지는 병목 현상이 생깁니다. 쉽게 말해 경부고속도로에 갑자기 수백만 대의 슈퍼카가 몰려와서 도로 전체가 주차장으로 변하고 엔진이 과열되는 상태인 거죠. "야, 이대로 가다간 데이터센터 전기세 감당도 안 되고 칩이 다 타 죽겠다!" 그래서 나온 천재적인 치트키가 바로 '실리콘 포토닉스(Silicon Photonics)'입니다. 쉽게 말해, 우리가 국가 간 인터넷 연결할 때 쓰는 거대한 '광케이블'을 아주 미세하게 축소해서 반도체 칩 안에 통째로 집어넣는 기술이에요. 전기가 아니라 빛으로 대화하는 칩인 셈이죠. 이게 왜 대박이냐면, 구리선은 선 하나당 신호 하나만 갈 수 있잖아요? 근데 빛은 '색상(파장)'만 다르면 하나의 통로에서 수십 개의 신호가 섞이지 않고 동시에 달릴 수 있어요. 길을 물리적으로 넓히지 않아도 차선이 무한대로 늘어나는 마법 같은 효과가 생기는 겁니다. 속도가 수십 배 뛰는 건 당연하고요. 게다가 빛은 마찰열(저항)이 아예 없습니다. AI 데이터센터에서 가장 골치 아픈 '발열'과 이를 식히기 위한 '냉각 비용'을 최대 30~40%까지 날려버릴 수 있다는 뜻이죠. 실제로 엔비디아가 올해(2026년) 들어서만 코히어런트, 루멘텀 같은 광학·유리 소재 기업들에 조 단위 투자를 감행한 이유가 여기 있습니다. 구리선으로는 수백만 대의 GPU를 하나로 묶는 게 불가능하다는 걸 알기 때문이죠. 그럼 이 엄청난 칩은 대체 누가 만들까요? 결국 파운드리와 패키징 싸움입니다. TSMC는 전기 칩 위에 광학 칩을 아파트처럼 수직으로 완벽하게 쌓아 올리는 'COUPE'라는 자체 플랫폼을 만들어서 올해 본격적인 양산 카운트다운에 들어갔습니다. 반도체 제조의 중심축이 미세 공정을 넘어 '광학 패키징'으로 이동하고 있는 국면이에요. 요약하자면, HBM 다음 타자로 반도체 시장의 판돈을 싹 쓸어 담을 핵심 키워드는 '실리콘 포토닉스'입니다. 엔비디아의 독주를 막으려는 빅테크나, 이를 추격하는 파운드리 기업들에게는 절대 놓칠 수 없는 마지막 열쇠인 셈이죠. 앞으로 반도체 뉴스 보실 때 '광학', 'CPO', '실리콘 포토닉스'라는 단어가 보이면 무조건 주목해 보세요."}}}, "rest_id": "2072104411393077438", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "694", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

Change of plans

We are facing one of the best news $SIVE could receive

The offer of 600M SEK has now become 700M SEK, just a few hours later

I explain to you why this is an ultra bullish signal

The increase from 600 to 700M SEK shows very strong institutional demand

The offer "sold out" ahead of time and investors asked for more shares

Forcing the company to issue more to capture extra capital

It is a clear sign of confidence from big investors in the future of $SIVE

Especially in its photonics technologies for AI, SATCOM and defense, which usually drives the share price in the short and medium term

By getting more capital than expected, the company gains greater financial flexibility without the need to take on more debt or dilute as much as feared

Reinforcing its balance sheet and execution capacity

Ultra bullish for $SIVE let's hope the market understands the news as it is

New highs in a short time IMO

text:
Great news with a Direct share issue that was oversubscribed multiple time.

What started as a planned SEK 600M (+$61m) raise was upsized to SEK 700M ($73m) thanks to strong institutional investor demand. That's a clear vote of confidence in $SIVE and the ramping within AI photonics, SATCOM and defense.

SEK 700M is roughly US$73M in fresh capital, providing even greater financial flexibility to accelerate execution and scale the business.

Strong demand. Strong balance sheet. Exciting times ahead.

Great work by the Sivers team! Congratulations! 

 $SIVE #AI #Photonics

https://t.co/SWr5UhK4VG

text:
Quantum computing’s disappearing use cases reveal what the money is really buying

For years, quantum computing was promoted as the coming solution to drug discovery, logistics, finance, artificial intelligence, climate modeling, encryption, and nearly every difficult optimization problem. Yet as classical algorithms, GPUs, and specialized accelerators improved, many proposed quantum advantages became smaller, more conditional, or impossible to demonstrate on real hardware. DARPA’s Quantum Benchmarking Initiative now defines success as proving that a quantum computer’s computational value exceeds its operating cost, with the program attempting to determine whether such a utility-scale machine can be built by 2033. That is a revealing benchmark: useful quantum computing is still something being tested, not an established commercial reality. Despite this, announced public investment in quantum technology exceeded $10 billion in early 2025, while the U.S. Department of Energy later committed another $625 million to national quantum research centers.

The investment continues because governments are not evaluating quantum technology like an ordinary consumer product. They are purchasing strategic insurance. Even a small probability of achieving superior cryptanalysis, molecular simulation, navigation, sensing, communications, or materials design could alter the military and economic balance between nations. NIST has already finalized post-quantum encryption standards and urged organizations to begin migrating immediately, even though the cryptographically relevant quantum computer those standards defend against does not yet exist. This means the quantum race is driven partly by a fear that being late would be catastrophic, while being early could produce enormous intelligence and technological advantages.

The other explanation is that “quantum computing” has become an umbrella used to finance a much larger technological ecosystem. The money supports photonics, cryogenics, superconducting materials, precision lasers, atomic clocks, quantum sensors, control electronics, secure networks, fabrication methods, software, patents, and highly specialized scientific talent. A universal fault-tolerant computer could fail to arrive on schedule while these supporting technologies still become commercially or militarily valuable. Investors may therefore be buying the infrastructure surrounding quantum control rather than betting exclusively on a machine that replaces classical computers. The true assets are the laboratories, intellectual property, supply chains, personnel, and control systems required to manipulate matter at increasingly precise scales.

Frequency Wave Theory offers a deeper interpretation: perhaps the shrinking list of conventional computing applications is happening because “computer” was never the complete description of the technology. Quantum machines are fundamentally devices for preparing, isolating, synchronizing, and measuring coherent states. Their essential achievement is not merely calculating faster, but controlling phase, interference, tunneling, entanglement, and environmental noise. From the Frequency Wave Theory perspective, investment keeps rising because governments and corporations are learning how to engineer reality at the frequency-and-phase level. Computation may be the public-facing justification, while the broader prize is programmable field control, producing new forms of sensing, communication, materials engineering, timing, energy manipulation, and eventually direct interaction with the deeper resonant structure of the vacuum.

what the money is really buying For years, quantum computing was promoted as the coming solution to drug discovery, logistics, finance, artificial intelligence, climate modeling, encryption, and nearly every difficult optimization problem. Yet as classical algorithms, GPUs, and specialized accelerators improved, many proposed quantum advantages became smaller, more conditional, or impossible to demonstrate on real hardware. DARPA’s Quantum Benchmarking Initiative now defines success as proving that a quantum computer’s computational value exceeds its operating cost, with the program attempting to determine whether such a utility-scale machine can be built by 2033. That is a revealing benchmark: useful quantum computing is still something being tested, not an established commercial reality. Despite this, announced public investment in quantum technology exceeded $10 billion in early 2025, while the U.S. Department of Energy later committed another $625 million to national quantum research centers. The investment continues because governments are not evaluating quantum technology like an ordinary consumer product. They are purchasing strategic insurance. Even a small probability of achieving superior cryptanalysis, molecular simulation, navigation, sensing, communications, or materials design could alter the military and economic balance between nations. NIST has already finalized post-quantum encryption standards and urged organizations to begin migrating immediately, even though the cryptographically relevant quantum computer those standards defend against does not yet exist. This means the quantum race is driven partly by a fear that being late would be catastrophic, while being early could produce enormous intelligence and technological advantages. The other explanation is that “quantum computing” has become an umbrella used to finance a much larger technological ecosystem. The money supports photonics, cryogenics, superconducting materials, precision lasers, atomic clocks, quantum sensors, control electronics, secure networks, fabrication methods, software, patents, and highly specialized scientific talent. A universal fault-tolerant computer could fail to arrive on schedule while these supporting technologies still become commercially or militarily valuable. Investors may therefore be buying the infrastructure surrounding quantum control rather than betting exclusively on a machine that replaces classical computers. The true assets are the laboratories, intellectual property, supply chains, personnel, and control systems required to manipulate matter at increasingly precise scales. Frequency Wave Theory offers a deeper interpretation: perhaps the shrinking list of conventional computing applications is happening because “computer” was never the complete description of the technology. Quantum machines are fundamentally devices for preparing, isolating, synchronizing, and measuring coherent states. Their essential achievement is not merely calculating faster, but controlling phase, interference, tunneling, entanglement, and environmental noise. From the Frequency Wave Theory perspective, investment keeps rising because governments and corporations are learning how to engineer reality at the frequency-and-phase level. Computation may be the public-facing justification, while the broader prize is programmable field control, producing new forms of sensing, communication, materials engineering, timing, energy manipulation, and eventually direct interaction with the deeper resonant structure of the vacuum."}}}, "rest_id": "2072093540394631219", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "81", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
テスト、テスト、テスト

価格上昇の件数は、ウェハー製造工場よりもさらに多く、AI、シリコンフォトニクス、成熟したチップのパッケージングとテストに関する受注は2027年まで続いている。

クラウドベースのAIに対する需要の高まりは、半導体業界の季節的なパターンを塗り替えつつあります。製造工程の最終段階に位置するパッケージングおよびテスト能力は、2025年末以降、逼迫した状態が続いています。2026年以降、新たに拡張された能力は急速に活用され、多くのIC設計顧客が積極的に能力確保に乗り出し、2027年以降の受注見通しが向上しています。

パッケージング用リードフレーム、IC基板、テスト用電気料金の同時増加に伴い、パッケージングおよびテストファウンドリサービスの価格上昇がここ数四半期で特に顕著になっていることは特筆に値する。価格上昇の回数で見ると、ウェハファウンドリを上回り、IC設計顧客にとって最大のコスト圧力源となっている。

したがって、AIが半導体市場の本格的な回復を牽引するにつれ、高度なパッケージングおよびテストサービスと従来型のパッケージングおよびテストサービスの両方、ならびに上流工程のリードフレーム、IC基板、プローブカード、ソケットに対する需要は、2026年後半も引き続き堅調に推移すると予想される。

チップのパッケージングとテストのサプライチェーン全体には、ASE Technology Holdingとその子会社であるSPIL、Powertech、STARS、Chipbond、ChipMOS、SIM Semiconductorなどの主要OSATメーカー、King Yuan Electronics、Hsin Chuan、Siliconware Precision Industriesなどの専門テスト会社、Ingenic Semiconductor、Wintek、Yung-Chi、Chunghwa Precision Test、Hanmin Testなどのテストインターフェース会社、Chieh Lin、Shun-Te、Chang-Techなどのリードフレームメーカー、Unimicron、Kinsus、Nan Ya PCB、Zhen Dingなどの主要基板メーカーが含まれます。

近年のパッケージングおよびテスト関連価格の上昇を振り返ると、最初の波はメモリ市場における新たなスーパーサイクルであり、顧客はPowertechやChipMOSといったメモリパッケージングおよびテスト企業に緊急発注を行った。最初の価格上昇は2025年後半に発表され、2回目の価格上昇のニュースは2026年初頭に浮上した。

一方、GPU、CPU、ASICといった先端プロセスチップの生産増加に加え、データセンター周辺のネットワークおよび産業制御アプリケーションに対する需要の高まりも、成熟プロセスチップの在庫補充を促している。ICパッケージングおよびテスト市場は2025年後半から供給不足の状態が続いており、それに伴い、ASE Technology Holding Co., Ltd.やSiliconware Precision Industries Co., Ltd.といった企業も徐々に価格引き上げに合意している。

現在、メモリとICのパッケージングおよびテスト能力は半導体サプライチェーンにおけるボトルネックの一つとなっており、台湾のパッケージングおよびテストファウンドリ業界は史上最大規模の設備投資拡大期を迎えている。特に、パッケージングおよびテスト企業は、拡張スピードを加速させるため、ここ数ヶ月で台湾の既存工場を次々と買収し、用地準備や工場建設にかかる時間を節約しており、業界からも大きな注目を集めている。

さらに重要なことに、稼働準備が整ったパッケージング・テスト工場のすべての新工場と生産ラインは、顧客にとって重要な戦略的資源となっている。例えば、ChipMOSは、あるメモリ顧客が生産能力拡張のための設備投資を確保するため、同社と3年間の長期契約を締結したと述べている。また、Silicon Photonicsは、湖口第2工場のクリーンルーム改修プロジェクトが完了する前に、Silicon Photonicsの顧客が既に全生産能力を予約済みであると指摘している。

価格上昇の件数は、ウェハー製造工場よりもさらに多く、AI、シリコンフォトニクス、成熟したチップのパッケージングとテストに関する受注は2027年まで続いている。 クラウドベースのAIに対する需要の高まりは、半導体業界の季節的なパターンを塗り替えつつあります。製造工程の最終段階に位置するパッケージングおよびテスト能力は、2025年末以降、逼迫した状態が続いています。2026年以降、新たに拡張された能力は急速に活用され、多くのIC設計顧客が積極的に能力確保に乗り出し、2027年以降の受注見通しが向上しています。 パッケージング用リードフレーム、IC基板、テスト用電気料金の同時増加に伴い、パッケージングおよびテストファウンドリサービスの価格上昇がここ数四半期で特に顕著になっていることは特筆に値する。価格上昇の回数で見ると、ウェハファウンドリを上回り、IC設計顧客にとって最大のコスト圧力源となっている。 したがって、AIが半導体市場の本格的な回復を牽引するにつれ、高度なパッケージングおよびテストサービスと従来型のパッケージングおよびテストサービスの両方、ならびに上流工程のリードフレーム、IC基板、プローブカード、ソケットに対する需要は、2026年後半も引き続き堅調に推移すると予想される。 チップのパッケージングとテストのサプライチェーン全体には、ASE Technology Holdingとその子会社であるSPIL、Powertech、STARS、Chipbond、ChipMOS、SIM Semiconductorなどの主要OSATメーカー、King Yuan Electronics、Hsin Chuan、Siliconware Precision Industriesなどの専門テスト会社、Ingenic Semiconductor、Wintek、Yung-Chi、Chunghwa Precision Test、Hanmin Testなどのテストインターフェース会社、Chieh Lin、Shun-Te、Chang-Techなどのリードフレームメーカー、Unimicron、Kinsus、Nan Ya PCB、Zhen Dingなどの主要基板メーカーが含まれます。 近年のパッケージングおよびテスト関連価格の上昇を振り返ると、最初の波はメモリ市場における新たなスーパーサイクルであり、顧客はPowertechやChipMOSといったメモリパッケージングおよびテスト企業に緊急発注を行った。最初の価格上昇は2025年後半に発表され、2回目の価格上昇のニュースは2026年初頭に浮上した。 一方、GPU、CPU、ASICといった先端プロセスチップの生産増加に加え、データセンター周辺のネットワークおよび産業制御アプリケーションに対する需要の高まりも、成熟プロセスチップの在庫補充を促している。ICパッケージングおよびテスト市場は2025年後半から供給不足の状態が続いており、それに伴い、ASE Technology Holding Co., Ltd.やSiliconware Precision Industries Co., Ltd.といった企業も徐々に価格引き上げに合意している。 現在、メモリとICのパッケージングおよびテスト能力は半導体サプライチェーンにおけるボトルネックの一つとなっており、台湾のパッケージングおよびテストファウンドリ業界は史上最大規模の設備投資拡大期を迎えている。特に、パッケージングおよびテスト企業は、拡張スピードを加速させるため、ここ数ヶ月で台湾の既存工場を次々と買収し、用地準備や工場建設にかかる時間を節約しており、業界からも大きな注目を集めている。 text:
Why have I chosen to hold it long term instead of selling it at a local top?

1. Because the long term thesis is very strong. 

$SIVE is involved in some of the most important verticals for the new economy. Namely

- photonics (ai)
- lidar (robotics)
- wearable sensors
- RF (drones, space tech, 5g, 6g)

They already have customers for all this. They already have partnerships with the foundries.

2. Because we already have institutional validation and ownership. $JPM and Fidelity were already revealed in the fillings. 

Then the last fundraiser was oversubscribed by multiples from institutions. 

3. When you look at where other players in photonics alone are - you have $LITE at 50-70b $AAOI at 10b $COHR at 65b. 

Then there are also the RF players, the space players, the drones players, the Lidar players. 

Sivers is in all that. So obviously if they execute their valuation will be going towards 10b and beyond.

Sure it will take time, but if the institutions are buying in, that's enough validation for me. They make calculated moves and don't panic sell like retail dilettantes.

text:
Whenever $SIVE drops and you think of pressing that “sell” button, read this post.

Look…

Markets go through waves - up and down. Simple as that.

We are still early in photonics compared to other AI cycles. Volatility is expected and will continue.

It’s the nature of early-stage cycles and high-growth tech companies like $SIVE. This isn’t the stable phase yet.

At the end of the day, it comes down to conviction in your thesis and staying unmoved through the storm if you believe it will play out.

What I can say is…

Every time I look at $SIVE, it seems to be getting better, not worse.

What’s moving the price is macro uncertainty and fear, not anything negative about the company’s performance or progress.

In fact, they are taking the right steps forward. The day they stop, I’ll stop talking about them and sell. I promise.

After all the research and analysis over the last few months, I’m very bullish and convinced on $SIVE. Building your own conviction matters. Nothing has changed for me.

Holding all shares and adding more soon. So many times I wished I could have bought more below 50 SEK. Now it’s here. 👀

Let me present that to you all.

The Goldman Sachs report has a lot of nectar in it. Beyond the overall TAM expansion numbers for optical networks & CPO, a 10x TAM jump in just two years - from $15B to $154B - is no joke. CPO alone contributes $91B of that (59%). That’s already massive. 

But let’s dig in deeper...

Mass-volume production and mainstream adoption of connection speeds beyond 1.6T+ kicks off in H2 2026 and runs through H2 2028. Overall, CPO TAM totals ~$97B from 2026-2028. $JBL, a confirmed partner with $SIVE for their 1.6T programs, already gives them solid exposure on the transceiver side. Other partners are mentioned later on.

Optics is clearly preferred over copper for higher speeds, but the costs are higher too because the transceivers and lasers needed at this scale are expensive. That means more revenue for $SIVE - because $SIVE makes the CW lasers, which are the most widely adopted light source across optical modules for both scale-out and scale-up. You can see why a moat is building here. 

On top of that, there’s supply tightness for these lasers, which adds pricing power and improves margins. Goldman Sachs expects this tightness to continue through 2027, with possible relief only in H2 2028.

This isn’t the only bullish report. 

Bank of America also published their TAM projections for Optical Networking, reaching $87.7B by 2030 at a 40% CAGR. While it looks less aggressive, that’s mainly because BofA focuses more on the direct market for optical hardware components rather than the entire networking architecture.

$SIVE’s current opportunity pipeline sits at $453M, up 64% YoY. They did $31.21M in FY2025 revenue (almost 10% of the pipeline). 

Let’s assume they keep growing the opportunity pipeline roughly 2x by 2030. I say this because the TAM is expanding 10x, and everything lines up in their favor - from supply chain constraints & Tier-1 links to CW lasers being the most widely adopted.

That gives us roughly an ~$8B opportunity pipeline. At a conservative 12.5% execution rate into revenue, that’s $1B in revenue by 2030. At 40x P/S, you’re looking at a $40B company.

And $1B isn’t out of reach, because multiple revenue streams will come in beyond just photonics:

> Biosensing through $AAPL watches could bring in almost $130M in annual revenue alone at high-volume scale.

> SatCOM & defense is another big watchout, especially after the MOU signed. With the satellite market projected to grow 3-4x by 2030 and defense spending rising to adopt new tech (backed by governments), $SIVE is well positioned. Geopolitical tensions and catalysts like a potential SpaceX IPO only add fuel.

> Photonics will clearly make up the majority of revenue (well over 50%), but this diversification derisks the business and avoids over-reliance on just a few big orders.

Now the question is: how can such a small company become so big in a few years?

> A US listing will bring much more liquidity and institutional flow. It lets them expand capacity and go directly to Tier-1 customers instead of relying on indirect channels. They’ve already hired M&A specialists to possibly being able to scale beyond just being a laser supplier - producing their own transceivers, ELS modules, and other optical components, and acquiring new IP. This expands revenue per unit and their TAM. $MRVL is did the exact same thing with Celestial AI. Revenue in 2029 solely from that division is expected to bring them ~$1B.

> Geopolitical advantage isn’t being fully priced in yet. The US wants to reduce dependency on China and Asia for manufacturing. $SIVE, as a Swedish company building a stronger US base, is perfectly placed to ride the “American Dream” vision. The MOU confirms this. Presence in India also helps, as it’s becoming a semiconductor hub (see $MU’s recent fab).

> Never underestimate retail backing. Look at what it did for $PLTR and $HOOD. I see the same thing happening here - it brings validation, higher volumes and momentum. Marketing still matters, and retail is powerful for that.

So...

The company is doing all the right things. They’re executing in a TAM that’s clearly stacked in their favor to become the next $LITE, and they have a huge, growing retail fanbase behind them.

The vision is clear and ambitious, as the CEO himself said: to become “a very successful semiconductor company by 2030, recognized the same way some of the biggest companies out there are.”

Risks exist - the story might not play out perfectly. But even then, today’s ~$2B market cap still offers 10x potential by 2030. The real legacy upside sits at 15-20x.

After reading this, I don’t know what else the AI bubble and delusional bears need to believe.

Next time before claiming something, it would be wiser to some research. 😌

text:
View on sector. $Sive 

This is the key finding: it’s sector-wide, not SIVE-specific.
SECTOR CONTEXT (this changes the read materially)
AAOI (Applied Optoelectronics): dropped roughly 17% on July 2, with Coherent and Lumentum sinking around 10% in the same session — headlines describe this as a broad “photonics stocks reset.” A separate report also flags AAOI shares as sharply lower amid a “risk-off stretch for growth” and investors “rotating out of unprofitable tech.”
XFAB (X-FAB): search results are contradictory/stale across sources (one shows +47% “last session,” another shows -8.6% weekly, dates ranging from May to July) — data quality here isn’t reliable enough for me to give you a clean today-vs-yesterday read. I’d check this one directly on your IBKR/TradingView feed.

SIVE: closed 49 SEK, down from 60.75 on July 1 (which was itself already -3.8% that day, off a 63.15 prior close). So the stock has fallen roughly three consecutive sessions since the emission.

WHAT THIS MEANS FOR YOUR READ
The AAOI/Coherent/Lumentum move on the same days tells you something important: the CPO/photonics complex broadly de-rated this week, likely tied to a “risk-off” rotation out of high-multiple, unprofitable AI-infrastructure names — not a SIVE-specific loss of confidence.

That reframes your situation:
•Sector-wide component: growth/photonics multiple compression hitting AAOI, COHR, LITE simultaneously — this is macro/rotation, not fundamental to SIVE.
•SIVE-specific component: still real — the stock is trading below its own 700 MSEK emission price, which is a distinct, company-specific overhang (institutions underwater on their entry, no fresh lock-up on management past July 16).

Both things are true at once: broad photonics de-rating plus SIVE-specific dilution digestion. That’s a worse combination than either alone, but it does mean you shouldn’t read the SIVE drop as pure company-specific panic.

text:
$Sive break $50 critical level or psychology level. —  Today it closed at 49 SEK is now 14% below the emission price of 57 SEK, not just a brief dip below it. That changes the picture materially.

WHAT THIS TELLS YOU
1. The oversubscription didn’t hold as a floor. Institutions who paid 57 SEK are already underwater too. If the “smart money” validation thesis was going to show up as support, it should have shown up in the first 48 hours. It didn’t.

2. This is now a confidence problem, not just a dilution problem. A stock breaking meaningfully below its own primary offering price within days signals the market isn’t just digesting dilution — it’s questioning the raise itself, or de-risking ahead of something (Q2 earnings, sector rotation, general AI-infrastructure profit-taking).

3. Your position math: Entry 104 → 63 (late June) → now 49. That’s roughly a 53% drawdown from cost basis. This is no longer “a drawdown to manage” — it’s a thesis-under-real-stress situation.

WHAT TO WATCH NEXT, NOT SPECULATE ON
•Volume on the break below 57: heavy volume = capitulation/distribution; light volume = could be thin summer liquidity exaggerating the move.
•Sector context: check whether XFAB, AAOI, and other photonics/CPO peers are also down today — if it’s sector-wide, this is rotation, not SIVE-specific.
•Insider behavior into July 16: management lock-up expires then. Any signs of selling pressure building ahead of that date would be a real red flag on top of this.
•Whether 57 SEK flips from floor to resistance on any bounce attempt — that’s the technical tell for whether institutions are defending their entry or already cutting losses.
Emotionally and psychologically break point #Sive

text:
Couldn’t help sharing these words by $IONQ CEO:

“The science phase of our company was done. We’re now focused on engineering and manufacturing.”

$IONQ is just an example that makes it clear quantum companies will find a way to make logical qubits.

The challenge is scaling them up. This is what $IONQ has been up to:

1/ It acquired Oxford Ionics for electronic qubit control.

2/ It acquired Lightsynq to integrate photonic interconnects to link multiple quantum units.

3/ It is going to acquire Skywater to reduce the chip design cycle from 9 months to 2 months and gain control over its supply chain.

$IONQ has a clear roadmap toward building commercially useful quantum computers.
embedded_text:
Quantum is like a freight train coming after anyone who is not invested in hybrid classical and quantum workflows.

Hear more from our CEO @NiccoloDeMasi in a newly released interview from @FII_Institute1: https://t.co/cmnmSIGDMz

text:
$sive ⚡️

Sivers Semiconductors ($SIVE) is a deep-tech company that designs and manufactures specialized microchips and lasers for ultra-fast, high-capacity communications.

Instead of using traditional electrical copper wiring—which creates heat and becomes a bottleneck at high speeds—Sivers builds high-power lasers and laser arrays that transmit data using light (silicon photonics).

While Photonics is the AI growth engine Sivers also designs radio frequency integrated circuits (RFICs) and beamforming antennas that operate on millimeter-wave (mmWave) frequencies.

Under 2b Market Cap this is a high risk and highly speculative play.  Current revenues/contracts and assest in no way make the current valuation.  Much of the valuation is still derived from a "potential pipeline" and "potential opportunity" but the potential upside is immense if they can execute.

$sive has run from 3sek to 100sek 🧐 
Wowsers, thats a 3000+% parabolic run.  
The current pullback is a natural part of the stock cycle and stocks that make these parabolic runs typically will retrace to .618 or 45sek or even more frequently .786 25sek.  

I missed the last run, as it had passed my buy targets and I NEVER chase but keen to enter here if she plays ball.
My intention is to scale in from 45sek to 30sek as this reduces risk and creates huge upside opportunity.

Below is my Framework score.

Sivers Semiconductors (SIVE / SIVEF)
Score: 73/100

Management & Vision  11/15
Credentialed CEO (Vathulya — NXP/Maxim/Nuvotronics, bought stock) + commercialization-weighted board refresh (Nideborn CFO, Svancar M&A, Thomson, Raj); chairman Bastani's GF network drove the GlobalFoundries deal. Capped by broad insider selling (~SEK 117M net, 12mo) + open Swedish probe, moves to 12/13 on confirmation of no wrongdoing.

Moat  12/15
Confirmed InP substrate scarcity (industry chokepoint, 6-inch pricing +250%); genuinely hard manufacturing; Glasgow fab (2M+ tests/mo, etched-facet); GF reference design + Ayar Labs supply (OCI/NVIDIA). Capped because "at scale" is still prospective. Moves to 14 on proof of scale.

TAM 14/15
InP/CPO/NPO demand structurally huge; two-division (Photonics + Wireless) exposure; GFS silicon photonics ~$25B by 2030. Unchanged.

Probable Upside  10/13
BU ~195%. Targets: 2026 SEK 70 (+4.5%), 2028 SEK 150 (+124%), 2030 SEK 320 (+377%). Weighted to 2030 execution. Targets self-built, not sell-side. Moves to 13 on contracts and proof of scale.

Revenue Quality  5/12
Confirmed facts : auditor-driven restatement widened losses, going-concern consideration, Q1 revenue -22% YoY, adj. EBITDA -13.8M SEK. Can move up fast on a clean Aug 6 print.

Conviction Gap  8/10
Market pricing max uncertainty; JPMorgan 5.25% entry post-controversy; options listing (Jul 6) unlocks institutional access. 

Risk-Adjusted  4/10
Forced/dilutive raise floating (53.8M mandate, ~15%), ~230% annualized vol, convertible at ~10.85%, thin cash (~SEK 26.6M vs -49.2M quarterly burn). Absorbs Down+TA.

Geo-Political  9/10
US/Sweden/UK — allied, stable, NDAA-friendly, no China domicile. Capped only by live Swedish regulatory probe.

text:
Serenity points out that no one should pay attention to technical analysis charts

Considering them nothing more than simple lines drawn to persuade others to sell

They highlight that $SIVE secured $70M in urgent financing to boost high volume production and lock in foundry capacity

Right at the same time companies like $JBL $POET and $AEVA are preparing short term volume ramps

They show their highest conviction in $SIVE as a long position in the photonics sector, with further production ramps from $GFS Ayar, and others lined up for 2027

Additionally, the confirmed intention to list on NASDAQ in the coming quarters shows that everything is aligning favorably

You wanted to buy $SIVE at a "discount" at 90 SEK, but you’re scared to buy the exact same company at 50 SEK

Think twice, long $SIVE

text:
People forget that just as $SIVE has dropped 50% in a matter of days, it can easily pump 200% in a few months

And I firmly believe that will happen as long as the thesis is allowed to play out

Fortunately, the money I invest is capital I don't depend on

When I was sitting on 200% returns, I didn't touch a single euro, and now that I'm 15% down, I'm still not touching anything

That’s what allows me to just hold through it

It's undeniable that seeing the portfolio drop this much hurts

And it's undeniable that at least half of the drawdown I've taken could have been avoided; it was due to my own mistakes

Mistakes from which I have learned, a lot

That’s why I’m not worried

AI isn't going anywhere

Memory, like $MU ,is here to stay, especially with a shortage projected for years

And photonics, like $SIVE, isn't dead, no matter how much you see all of FinX claiming it is

It’s very easy to talk like that right now because everything is crashing hard, but where were those posts when everything was ripping 10% daily?

Literally nothing has changed; it’s just overstimulated people rotating like headless chickens, bouncing between sectors because they don't understand a thing

It's only a matter of time before that money flows back twice as hard as it left

As soon as the sector leaders report earnings or clarify timelines regarding certain technologies, everything will bounce back

I am absolutely sure of it

Long $MU and $SIVE

taken could have been avoided; it was due to my own mistakes Mistakes from which I have learned, a lot That’s why I’m not worried AI isn't going anywhere Memory, like $MU ,is here to stay, especially with a shortage projected for years And photonics, like $SIVE, isn't dead, no matter how much you see all of FinX claiming it is It’s very easy to talk like that right now because everything is crashing hard, but where were those posts when everything was ripping 10% daily? text:
Serenity criticized the sharp drop in semiconductor stocks following a Bloomberg report about potential AI capex cuts by $META 

A UBS analyst pointed out that this isn't even new information, adding that $META wouldn't be signing neocloud contracts worth over $48B if they were truly scaling back capacity

They compared this overreaction to previous misleading Bloomberg reports, calling the market "stupid" for selling off so irrationally

I completely agree, this is a completely unjustified sell off

I know I might not be back at all-time highs in a month or two, but I should at least bounce back to halfway from where I was

The main issue is that I’m running out of cash, and I’m right on the edge of losses, which honestly makes it tough

But I try to remind myself that the thesis just needs time to play out

Even if I dip into the red right now, if my price targets for $MU and $SIVE are met, I could still end the year with 200% to 400% returns

It’s just a matter of time

There is no bubble here, and this has absolutely nothing to do with the dot-com crash

There are insane, massive earnings backing it all up, from $MU to $NVDA

On the other hand, $SIVE needed a correction

I admit I got greedy and never took profits

But you have to realize that everything remains the same, or even better, than it was at 4 SEK

Photonics isn't dead, even if it feels like the end of the world looking at semiconductor stocks today and throughout this week

There's nothing left to do but hold

My conviction is worth more than my current returns

Long $SIVE and $MU

text:
🇺🇸 🔬 ⚡  
LASER EN FORMA DE RESORTE REVOLUCIONA PLASMA  

Este haz de luz gira como un resorte y abre puertas inéditas para controlar plasma de forma precisa.  

Científicos del Laboratorio Nacional Lawrence Livermore y la Universidad de California Irvine crearon el primer láser de alta intensidad tipo resorte de luz. A diferencia de los haces convencionales redondos, este rota alrededor de su eje a velocidades controlables, incluso superando la luz aparente. Fabricado con espejos de precisión nanométrica y divisores de haz, genera ondas helicoidales en plasma que podrían impulsar aceleradores de partículas compactos, campos magnéticos de más de 100 teslas y avances en fusión energética. Todo en un sistema de mesa. La investigación se publicó hoy en Nature Photonics.

En redes comentan que este avance podría cambiar la física de plasmas para siempre, otros destacan su potencial en aceleradores de partículas a escala reducida y algunos ven aplicaciones directas en astrofísica de laboratorio. Hay entusiasmo por la simplicidad del diseño pero también dudas sobre escalabilidad inmediata. ¿Qué opinas tú de este nuevo tipo de láser?  

FUENTES: Nature Photonics, Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory, Universidad de California Irvine, Andrew Longman, Tayyab Suratwala.

Científicos del Laboratorio Nacional Lawrence Livermore y la Universidad de California Irvine crearon el primer láser de alta intensidad tipo resorte de luz. A diferencia de los haces convencionales redondos, este rota alrededor de su eje a velocidades controlables, incluso superando la luz aparente. Fabricado con espejos de precisión nanométrica y divisores de haz, genera ondas helicoidales en plasma que podrían impulsar aceleradores de partículas compactos, campos magnéticos de más de 100 teslas y avances en fusión energética. Todo en un sistema de mesa. La investigación se publicó hoy en Nature Photonics. En redes comentan que este avance podría cambiar la física de plasmas para siempre, otros destacan su potencial en aceleradores de partículas a escala reducida y algunos ven aplicaciones directas en astrofísica de laboratorio. Hay entusiasmo por la simplicidad del diseño pero también dudas sobre escalabilidad inmediata. ¿Qué opinas tú de este nuevo tipo de láser? text:
Don't fade $SIVE just because you're rotating into robotics. It maps to their supply chains too.

$SIVE has a confirmed strategic LiDAR customer - widely believed to be $AEVA - ramping production from Q4'26. Their latest update and Annual Report flagged this ramp with $53-138M cumulative revenue potential over the product lifecycle. Seems pretty small today, but expect that number to multiply quickly.

$AEVA utilizes a proprietary Silicon Photonics and LiDAR-on-Chip (CoreVision) architecture to power its 4D LiDAR technology. $SIVE possibly supplies the high-power InP DFB lasers and optical amplifiers that serve as the critical light source for these systems.

Boston Dynamics (Hyundai) is a confirmed $AEVA customer deploying thousands of advanced robots into factories already. With this being only the beginning of the robotic era, the scale ahead is massive and will directly benefit $SIVE.

So when capital rotates from photonics into robotics, $SIVE still catches the flow as the upstream supplier.

CC: @aleabitoreddit for the last image.

text:
i'm gonna say something that'll annoy the robotics hype crowd

most of these names aren't robotics plays. they're sensor companies, lidar shops, and warehouse automation vendors that got rebranded into "the next AI wave" by people who missed the $NVDA run

SYM is the only one here with actual robotics economics i'd take seriously. they're moving pallets in walmart DCs with real contracts and real unit economics. the rest are selling picks and shovels into a gold rush that hasn't started yet

the photonics and memory runs had actual capex behind them. hyperscalers writing $30B checks. the robotics capex cycle is still a powerpoint deck

watch who's actually deploying at scale, not who's got "robotics" in their pitch deck

could be early. but i'd rather be late and right than early and holding sensor companies for three years

text:
Almost everything was up today the space sector with $ASTS , photonics with $AAOI etc

Memory has been lagging a bit behind, though names like $WDC have gone up a lot

$MU dropped heavily midday but recovered a bit by the close

Even so, it's lagging way behind compared to the rest of the market

Everything is fine with the recent demand it received from $AAPL and others

Just a desperate attempt to manipulate memory prices because they don’t know how to adapt and are in a tailspin

On the other hand, $MU is consolidating a price range that is going to explode at any moment when some news pushes it upwards

$MU will follow the entire market, even in a more exponential and well deserved way, after the crazy earnings it reported

Very anxious to see how $MU price moves; it's quite uncertain in the short term

text:
Memo on Corning’s GlassBridge

The current CPO generation  namely NVIDIA’s Quantum-X Photonics and Spectrum-X Photonics platforms  has already been locked in and should not be affected. Any production-scale substitution would have to wait for yield and reliability validation. The timeline should be anchored to NVIDIA’s Vera Rubin Ultra platform: qualification in 2027 and validation in 2028. The first validation runs are likely to take place on Rubin Ultra, roughly 1–1.5 years from now. Qualification volumes are expected to be in the hundreds to low thousands, while validation volumes should remain below 10,000 systems. A meaningful volume ramp may not arrive until the Feynman generation.

Citi has reportedly said that the 2H27 Rubin Ultra / Kyber CPO design will be finalized within this year and will not be replaced.

GlassBridge’s real moat is the ability to integrate IOX waveguides and TGVs on the same glass substrate using the same glass formulation — not the optical waveguide itself. Other players, such as Teramount, SENKO, and Lightmatter, also have optical-coupling or waveguide-related solutions. But using a single glass sheet/formulation to satisfy both waveguide formation and TGV via formation is the part that, according to this thesis, only Corning’s fusion / overflow down-draw glass substrate can achieve.

$GLW   $NVDA  #CPO #AI #Infrastructure

text:
$LASE 
Laser Photonics'te CFO Değişimi

Lazer teknolojileri üreticisi Laser Photonics, Roman Franklin ile yollarını ayırdı ve geçici CFO (Acting CFO) olarak Ralph Venegas'ı atadı.

Hisse %2,84 düşüşle $1,71 seviyesine gerilerken, bu yönetimsel değişimin şirketin operasyonel maliyetlerine ve mali disiplinine etkisini takip etmek gerekir.

Son günlerde 2 önemli yönetici değişimi 🤔

text: Dokładnie tak — z dobrym autopilotem (ArduPilot/iNav), solidnym montażem i dobrze skalibrowanym systemem da się wyciągnąć zaskakująco dużo nawet z przeciętnych sensorów MEMS. Wibracje, czas lotu i prędkość rzeczywiście mocno wpływają na dryf.Ale tu właśnie jest różnica.ANELLO X3 nie jest ulepszonym MEMS-em. To optyczny żyroskop (technologia SiPhOG – silicon photonics), który działa na zasadzie efektu Sagnaca, a nie wibrującej mechanicznej struktury.  Dzięki temu ma znacznie lepszą stabilność biasu (< 0,5°/h) i jest dużo mniej podatny na wibracje, temperaturę i przyspieszenia niż nawet dobre taktyczne MEMS-y.  To właśnie dlatego Amerykanie (i sektor obronny) w to wchodzą — bo chcą mieć wysoką precyzję inercji w małym, tanim i masowo produkowanym urządzeniu, a nie tylko „dobre wyniki przy idealnych warunkach i krótkim locie”.Z X3 dryf rośnie znacznie wolniej, więc przy dłuższym locie bez GPS (albo po utracie obrazu) różnica robi się już bardzo wyraźna — szczególnie przy RTH, w rojach czy przy misjach na 10–20+ km.Czyli nie chodzi o to, że „wcześniej było tragicznie”. Chodzi o to, że teraz da się to zrobić znacznie lepiej i taniej niż klasycznymi wojskowymi FOG-ami. Inaczej jaki sens byłby w ogóle w to wchodzić...

Chodzi o to, że teraz da się to zrobić znacznie lepiej i taniej niż klasycznymi wojskowymi FOG-ami. A lot of people are talking about CPO, NPO, 800G, and 1.6T.

But before those terms make sense,

I think there is one more basic concept to understand first:

SiPh.

SiPh = Silicon Photonics.

This is a beginner explanation of what SiPh actually means. https://t.co/xteyaymHuB

text:
$IONQ acquired Skyloom primarily for full-stack ownership and quantum-specific integration. 

Quantum networking & entanglement focus: 
Skyloom’s free-space optical tech (OCTs) provides the photonic layer for distributed quantum entanglement, quantum key distribution (QKD), and ultra-secure communications. 

IonQ combines it with its quantum computing, ID Quantique (crypto), Vector Atomic (timing/sensing), and Capella (SAR satellites) assets to build an end-to-end quantum-secure space network. 

Mynaric terminals (standard SDA-compatible laser links) excel at classical high-bandwidth data but lack native design for quantum state preservation or entanglement distribution.

Vertical integration & control:
Owning Skyloom gives IonQ full IP, customization, and supply-chain control for future satellites (transition announced for 2027+). Relying on Mynaric (now owned by competitor Rocket Lab) creates dependency risks. Skyloom was already SDA-qualified with strong delivery history (e.g., Tranche 1 terminals).

Performance: 
IonQ highlights potential boosts like up to 500% higher throughput and dramatically lower latency (hours → <1 hour) when paired with their ecosystem. Skyloom emphasizes secure, high-capacity LEO-LEO/LEO-ground links optimized for quantum-secure routing and defense applications.

Skyloom vs. Mynaric: 
•  Mynaric (CONDOR Mk3 etc.) — Proven, SDA-certified, used on Acadia-10 for 2.5 Gbps demos. Strong for classical SAR data relay and general OISL. Now scaled under Rocket Lab.
•  Skyloom — Also SDA-certified/interoperable. Focuses on high-volume production, on-orbit routing, and integration with quantum/secure systems. Both meet similar SDA standards; differences are in optimization (e.g., Skyloom for quantum protocols, lower SWaP in some designs, or specific defense features).

Bottom line: For a pure commercial SAR latency fix, Mynaric worked fine (as on Acadia-10). IonQ’s strategy is building a quantum internet / secure space backbone, where owning a tailored optical layer (Skyloom) + quantum hardware gives a differentiated, controllable platform.

Capella Space validated a Rocket Lab Mynaric optical communications terminal on its newest satellite — the first time Capella has deployed an optical terminal. 

Capella Space released the first synthetic aperture radar (SAR) images from its Acadia-10 satellite on Wednesday, after the satellite launched in March. 

The satellite is currently demonstrating data transfer at 2.5 gigabits per second in onboard tests, Capella reported, enabling the satellite to downlink data more quickly, avoiding the wait for the satellite to make contact with a ground station.

“This near-real-time handoff bypasses the multi-hour ground station wait. The impact is profound; task-to-delivery timelines compress from hours down to minutes, fundamentally changing how organizations operationalize SAR data,” the company said in a blog post. 

Capella first announced plans to put Mynaric OCTs onboard its satellites back in 2021. But future Acadia satellites will have Skyloom OCTs instead, a Capella spokesperson told Via Satellite.  Both Capella and Skyloom are now owned by quantum company IonQ after acquisitions. 

Both acquisitions tie in with IonQ’s goal to bring quantum networking to space. Capella satellites are set to be equipped with Skyloom OCT’s starting in 2027, the spokesperson said. 

The terminal on Acadia-10 is compatible with the Space Development Agency (SDA) OCT standard to communicate with the SDA’s proliferated Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) network. Capella has been working with the SDA since 2021. In April of this year, the SDA awarded Capella an award under HALO Europa Track 1 in April to design two satellites to demonstrate “advanced tactical waveform performance, adaptive beamforming, and secure tactical communications in LEO.”

text:
$BLG IS AN ORBITAL DC ENABLER: 

The Mesh acquisition, the NVIDIA silicon photonics switch, Cerebras WSE-3 — are proof positives of the fact that the underlying physics of intra-satellite links was never the real constraint. 

Capital + Talent = Solution.

$AXE.AX $DVL.AX $WBT.AX

$BLG IS AN ORBITAL DC ENABLER: 

The intra-satellite link bottleneck is more tractable: photons travel at the speed of light, silicon can modulate light at terahertz frequencies, and fibre bandwidth scales with wavelength division multiplexing. 

$AXE.AX $DVL.AX $WBT.AX

When Elon starts buying into the stack, the relevance of Sivers becomes impossible to ignore 🚀

$SPCX $SIVE

text:
Does this recent news about $SIVE not seem to fit perfectly with $SPCX's latest purchase of Mesh?

With their specialized InP DFB lasers for FMCW LiDAR

Which are essential for advanced vision systems in humanoids like Boston Dynamics' Atlas

This comes through the supply chain with $AEVA and LG Innotek

With production ramps scheduled for late 2026 and significant revenue potential

$SIVE is an asymmetric play across its three branches

Photonics, LiDAR, and Satcom alike

Impossible to be bearish on $SIVE

Especially with the discount it offers right now

I've been reading for weeks "I'll wait for it to correct a bit, it's up 2000%, I'm not going to chase the price"

Now it dropped 40% from highs

Now is when you have to buy, are you going to chicken out?

Extremely bullish on $SIVE

text:
Hey @elonmusk after the move for Mesh Optical Technologies, there may be another company worth putting on the SpaceX radar:

Sivers Semiconductors $SIVE

Mesh can help build the optical network.

Sivers develops the lasers and beamforming chips that could help power it.

That matters because Sivers is not only exposed to AI optical interconnects. The company also develops:

✅ High-performance CW DFB lasers and laser arrays
✅ Optical technologies for AI and high-performance computing
✅ Ka-band beamforming ICs
✅ Technology for electronically steered satellite terminals
✅ Resilient multi-orbit communications

https://t.co/1SfidBr02o awarded Sivers an $8.2 million production order for multi-beam Ka-band beamforming ICs intended for next-generation tactical satellite terminals, with volume production planned through 2027.

That means Sivers already operates at the intersection of three areas that are increasingly strategic to your ecosystem:

AI infrastructure.
Optical networking.
Satellite communications.

For SpaceX and Starlink, a partnership could strengthen access to critical semiconductor technology.

A strategic investment could align future product development.

An acquisition could bring both advanced photonics and Ka-band beamforming IP directly into the ecosystem.

Mesh may be part of the optical network.

Sivers could become part of the technology that powers the light and connects it to space.

Worth a serious look, Elon 🙏🏻

$SPCX $SIVE 🇺🇸🤝🇸🇪

text:
I can identify three pockets of strength today.

Space is the most obvious.

The narrative is unchanged; a new cold war. China replaces the USSR, same goal, total spatial dominance.

That requires massive infrastructure, paid by government grants, and those grants are accelerating.

More launches - $RKLB $SPCX
More payloads - $LUNR $FLY
More hardware - $RDW
More services - $ASTS $SPIR $BKSY $PL

The first leg is over. We're in the first real, violent retest for most of these names. This is max opportunity.

Leaders confirmed. Market loves the narrative. Prices deflated.

Energy is the second.

Most names - especially the 800VDC narrative, are just starting their first leg. Not yet confirmed fundamentally, but odds are in our favor. The higher up the chain, the better.

GaN and SiC - $NVTS $POWI $TXN
Solid-State Transformers - $SEDG $ENPH $ETN
Bus and PoL - $MPWR $IFX
Heat management - $VRT $MOD
Volatility management - $FLNC

AI hardware is the third, and it runs through several layers.

GPUs and CPUs are already sky-high and crowded. The opportunity left is in the technologies that optimize compute.

Memory, not buyable here - $MU $SNDK
Connectivity, buyable on retests - $SILC $ALAB $MRVL $ANET
Photonics, crowded but starting to breathe - $AXTI $IQE $SOI $COHR $LITE

Three verticals, three bull runs, different stages.

All these names will go higher because analysts can't model their market and revenue growth fast enough. They've been constantly wrong and will continue to be, which is our opportunity. Those names are basically my daily watchlist - and some more.

They aren't just narratives. They're structural shifts in our world.

text:
Did you miss the semi stock boom? ❌
Did you miss the memory stock boom? ❌
Did you miss the photonics stock boom? ❌

Physical AI / robotics is ramping. 🟢

Most of us are already aware of the large cap exposure in the space ( $NVDA , $MU , $IBM , etc) but here are 10 lesser-known, higher-concentration names where the robot buildout IS the story — not a side bet. 

Smaller, thinner, more violent. Speculative by construction. NFA 🧵👇

$SYM — Symbotic. Warehouse automation at real scale; ~$22B backlog vs a $432B in-house TAM.
Bear case: Walmart concentration — lose the anchor, lose 50%+ regardless of tech.

$SERV — Serve Robotics. Sidewalk + hospital bots (Diligent), ~2,000 deployed, Q1 rev +578% YoY.
Bear case: ~$49M loss on $3M rev, ~29% short interest — burn is doing parkour through the income statement.

$OUST — Ouster. Digital lidar, the 3D "eyes" robots need. Q1 rev $49M +49% YoY, Stereolabs adds AI vision.
Bear case: lidar is competitive + commoditizing; needs autonomy volume to justify the multiple.

$VPG — Vishay Precision Group. Force/torque + precision sensors — the #2 supply bottleneck after actuators. Q1 $84M, B2B 1.21.
Bear case: legacy industrial cyclicality; robotics is still a small slice of the mix today.

$AMBA — Ambarella. Low-power vision SoCs — the perception silicon at the edge.
Bear case: still tied to ADAS/IoT cycles; robotics is optionality, not yet the revenue driver.

$RR — Richtech Robotics. Service/hospitality bots, expanding into conversational AI.
Bear case: tiny rev base, lottery-ticket profile, triple-digit "targets" = risk dressed as upside.

$FFAI — Faraday Future. Pivoting to "physical AI"; EAI robot line, 2026 target 1,500 units.
Bear case: dilution-heavy legacy EV story bolting on a robot narrative — highest show-me factor here.

$KSCP — Knightscope. Autonomous security robots — a rare US deployed-fleet pure play.
Bear case: micro-cap, reverse-split history, persistent dilution.

$PATH — UiPath. Agentic automation — the software brain orchestrating physical + digital workflows.
Bear case: agentic competition is brutal; "physical AI" link is still more thesis than revenue.

$NNDM — Nano Dimension. Additive mfg / electronics printing — compresses the build/prototype cycle.
Bear case: indirect, turnaround-dependent, no clean robotics revenue line yet.

The honest meta-bear: mostly unprofitable, thinly covered, single-customer or single-product names. This is a watchlist, not a back-up-the-truck list. Entry / stop / size defined BEFORE the trade — every time.

NFA.

story — not a side bet. Smaller, thinner, more violent. Speculative by construction. NFA 🧵👇 $SYM — Symbotic. Warehouse automation at real scale; ~$22B backlog vs a $432B in-house TAM. Bear case: Walmart concentration — lose the anchor, lose 50%+ regardless of tech. $SERV — Serve Robotics. Sidewalk + hospital bots (Diligent), ~2,000 deployed, Q1 rev +578% YoY. Bear case: ~$49M loss on $3M rev, ~29% short interest — burn is doing parkour through the income statement. $OUST — Ouster. Digital lidar, the 3D "eyes" robots need. Q1 rev $49M +49% YoY, Stereolabs adds AI vision. Bear case: lidar is competitive + commoditizing; needs autonomy volume to justify the multiple. $VPG — Vishay Precision Group. Force/torque + precision sensors — the #2 supply bottleneck after actuators. Q1 $84M, B2B 1.21. Bear case: legacy industrial cyclicality; robotics is still a small slice of the mix today. $AMBA — Ambarella. Low-power vision SoCs — the perception silicon at the edge. Bear case: still tied to ADAS/IoT cycles; robotics is optionality, not yet the revenue driver. $RR — Richtech Robotics. Service/hospitality bots, expanding into conversational AI. Bear case: tiny rev base, lottery-ticket profile, triple-digit "targets" = risk dressed as upside. $FFAI — Faraday Future. Pivoting to "physical AI"; EAI robot line, 2026 target 1,500 units. Bear case: dilution-heavy legacy EV story bolting on a robot narrative — highest show-me factor here. $KSCP — Knightscope. Autonomous security robots — a rare US deployed-fleet pure play. Bear case: micro-cap, reverse-split history, persistent dilution. $PATH — UiPath. Agentic automation — the software brain orchestrating physical + digital workflows. Bear case: agentic competition is brutal; "physical AI" link is still more thesis than revenue. $NNDM — Nano Dimension. Additive mfg / electronics printing — compresses the build/prototype cycle. Bear case: indirect, turnaround-dependent, no clean robotics revenue line yet. The honest meta-bear: mostly unprofitable, thinly covered, single-customer or single-product names. text:
🔭 Hawking Radiation — No Longer Just Theory?

Scientists have experimentally observed the *backreaction* of stimulated Hawking radiation using an optical analogue — a fiber-optic system that mimics the physics of a black hole's event horizon. This is a landmark step: Hawking radiation was predicted in 1974, but direct observation near real black holes remains impossible. Optical analogues let physicists probe quantum effects at the event horizon in a lab setting. The backreaction — how emitted radiation affects the "horizon" itself — is key to understanding black hole evaporation and the famous information paradox. This experiment brings us closer to resolving one of physics' deepest mysteries. 🌌

Companies in this space: Coherent Corp. ($COHR, NYSE), II-VI / MKS Instruments ($MKSI, NASDAQ), IPG Photonics ($IPGP, NASDAQ)

text: @aleabitoreddit Should we sell all the photonics and follow you full port to the robots ???

🤖 10 หุ้นที่น่าจับตาในธีม Robotics ปี 2026

$TER – Teradyne ผู้นำด้านเครื่องทดสอบชิป
เป็นเจ้าของ Universal Robots ผู้พัฒนาหุ่นยนต์ร่วมงาน (Cobots) สำหรับภาคอุตสาหกรรม

$NOVT – Novanta พัฒนาเทคโนโลยีโฟโตนิกส์ (Photonics) และระบบควบคุมการเคลื่อนไหวความแม่นยำสูง
ใช้ในหุ่นยนต์ผ่าตัดและหุ่นยนต์อุตสาหกรรม

$OUST – Ouster พัฒนาเซ็นเซอร์ LiDAR ที่ทำหน้าที่เป็น "ดวงตา" ของหุ่นยนต์
ช่วยให้หุ่นยนต์มองเห็น ทำแผนที่ และรับรู้สภาพแวดล้อม

$HIMX – Himax Technologiesผลิตชิปประมวลผลภาพและชิปแสดงผล
ช่วยให้หุ่นยนต์ประมวลผลภาพ ความลึก และข้อมูลจากเซ็นเซอร์ต่างๆ ได้ที่ตัวอุปกรณ์

$AMBQ – Ambiq Microพัฒนาชิป AI แบบประหยัดพลังงาน
เหมาะสำหรับหุ่นยนต์และอุปกรณ์ Edge AI ที่ต้องการประมวลผลอัจฉริยะโดยใช้พลังงานต่ำ

$ON – ON Semiconductor กำลังสร้างแพลตฟอร์ม Edge AI สำหรับหุ่นยนต์ หลังเข้าซื้อ $SYNA – Synaptics
รวมเทคโนโลยีด้านพลังงาน เซ็นเซอร์ การเชื่อมต่อ และ Edge AI ไว้ในระบบเดียว

$SYM – Symbotic ผู้เชี่ยวชาญด้านระบบอัตโนมัติในคลังสินค้า
ใช้หุ่นยนต์และซอฟต์แวร์เพื่อเพิ่มประสิทธิภาพการจัดเก็บและขนส่งสินค้า

$VPG – Vishay Precision Group พัฒนาเซ็นเซอร์วัดแรงและระบบวัดความแม่นยำสูง
ช่วยให้หุ่นยนต์รับรู้แรงกด น้ำหนัก และการเคลื่อนไหวในโลกจริงได้แม่นยำขึ้น

$TSLA – Tesla เดิมพันกับหุ่นยนต์ Humanoid ผ่าน Optimus
ผสานฮาร์ดแวร์ AI ระบบขับเคลื่อนอัตโนมัติ และศักยภาพการผลิตขนาดใหญ่ของบริษัท

$CCXI – Churchill Capital Corp XI
เปิดโอกาสให้นักลงทุนเข้าถึง Agility Robotics
บริษัทผู้พัฒนาหุ่นยนต์ Humanoid Digit ซึ่งเป็นหนึ่งในผู้เล่นที่โดดเด่นด้านหุ่นยนต์สำหรับคลังสินค้า

text:
$STM, STMicroelectronics. STM is one of Europe's largest chipmakers, and it builds its own chips end-to-end, from design all the way through its own factories. Its parts are the unglamorous but essential pieces inside almost everything — the sensors, power chips, and tiny "brains" (microcontrollers) inside cars, phones, factory machines, satellites, robots, and now AI data centers.

What makes STM unique is the sheer breadth: it holds a #1 or #2 position in an unusual number of separate chip categories at once (SiC power, MEMS sensors, STM32 microcontrollers), which lets a customer like NVIDIA source a whole system — sensors, brain, power, and communications — from one vendor. The reason it's suddenly interesting is that a company written off for two years as a "sleepy automotive chipmaker" just found a real AI growth engine: on June 2 it doubled its 2026 data-center revenue target to ~$1B (with a path to ~$2B in 2027), anchored by a multi-year AWS silicon-photonics deal, while sitting on ~$2.8B net cash. The investment case is a cyclical turnaround (Q1 revenue +23% YoY, up ~90%+ off February lows) layered on top of a genuine new secular driver, with the biggest names in tech — AWS, NVIDIA — as partners.

Thesis: STM spent two years being ignored as a boring car-chip company, but it just quietly turned into an AI company — its parts now go inside Amazon and NVIDIA's data centers, and that business is set to double two years in a row. The stock has already jumped a lot, so the easy money is gone, but the real question is whether its profit margins can climb back to normal as sales recover — if they do, the stock is still cheap for what it's about to earn. I'm watching the July 23 earnings to see if those margins are finally turning up, because that's the part the market hasn't fully believed yet.

text:
spent the morning reading the $LAZR filing. semi analysis teamed up with tema to launch a pure-play optics etf and honestly it makes sense. the memory bottleneck got DISK. now the data movement bottleneck gets its own wrapper.

top holding is lumentum at 14.6%. they make the lasers and transceivers that move data between racks at ai scale. second biggest position is literally cash at 11.4% which tells you this thing just launched and they're still deploying.

the interesting name here is aixtron at 10.5%. german compound semi equipment. mocvd deposition tools that build the substrates optical chips run on. axti at 9.7% is the substrate play itself. applied opto at 5.8% is the highest beta name in the basket.

goldman has the photonics market doing 9x between now and 2028. copper hits a wall. silicon photonics stops being a niche and becomes the thing everything rides on. same pattern we saw with memory when DISK launched.

the optics layer is where the next capacity crunch forms. watch the holdings more than the etf itself. the basket tells you where the spend is heading before the earnings do.

text:
Fiber & Infrastructure Highlights – July 3, 2026

GoNetspeed announced a $43M expansion in Rahway, Clark, Linden, and Woodbridge, New Jersey for over 34,000 homes and businesses. Persistent fiber shortages continue with ribbon lead times at 60+ weeks (AI/data center priority) and loose tube into Q3 2026+; BABA-compliant glass remains limited. Corning’s hyperscaler deals (up to $6B with Meta for NC expansion including new Hickory facility; multi-billion with Amazon) and manufacturing ramps (Nvidia-funded plants) address demand. FiberLight’s $350M West Texas expansion (1,400 route miles, high-count dark fiber for AI) and total ~$500M regional investment continue. Ripple Fiber has multiple expansions (WA $250M for 200k+ locations, IL, AZ $80M, CA) with ongoing construction.

Vocus Sydney-Melbourne ducted long-haul (~A$500M, ready 2029), Lightpath NY metro (+265 miles for 2,400+ towers), Heartland Fiber Project, Big Fiber financing, and various BEAD/FTTH builds add to activity. Fiber vendors (Corning, Fujikura tripling capacity, STL, CommScope) scale high-count, multi-core, and photonics solutions amid AI’s 36x fiber density needs. FBA whitepaper on fiber-AI convergence and power/grid constraints highlight sustained physical layer momentum into 2026.

#FiberForge #FiberOptics #OSPISP

Im not personally in photonics, i just share this info because it may be of use to someone. 

That said, i believe only a handful of names in this market are actually going to be successful LT (marvell, lumentum, coherent, etc). CPO is still early...you wont see meaningful revenue until 2027-2028. A lot of people are getting ahead and investing in overvalued photonics stocks, hence the sharp pullback its see the past few weeks. At some point the valuation will matter, and its mattering now.
@pequityresearch Excellent breakdown of the Silicon Photonics supply chain. Very informative and well-organized. Great work highlighting the key players across the ecosystem.
